content.text: HDR Output changes the D3D12 proxy swap chain format and color space for scRGB or HDR10 display output.
Recommended quick start: choose scRGB on Windows 11 for native HDR and leave the luminance values at their defaults.
Use HDR10 when you want standard PQ HDR output and your display advertises HDR10 support.
Use Open Calibration Preview to launch an ImGui overlay with real-time HDR test patterns and luminance sliders.
Changes take effect after restarting the game.

content.help: 0 disables HDR output. scRGB is the Win11-native, best compatibility FP16 color space. HDR10 uses PQ HDR with HDR10 metadata and requires display HDR10 support. Changes take effect after restart.

HDR10 only. Set this to your display peak brightness in nits. Check the display spec sheet or Windows Advanced Display peak brightness. Common values: HDR600=600, OLED TV=800, HDR1000/QD-OLED=1000-2000. Use 1000 if unsure. Changes take effect after restart.

HDR10 only. Controls SDR UI, text, and HUD brightness inside the HDR frame. Start at 200. Try 80-150 in a dark room, 250-400 in a bright room. Too high can make HUD elements glare. Changes take effect after restart.

scRGB only. Defines the physical luminance represented by linear scRGB white (1.0, 1.0, 1.0). 80 nits is the sRGB standard reference white and should usually stay unchanged unless you have calibration requirements. Changes take effect after restart.
Opens an ImGui overlay with HDR test patterns and real-time luminance sliders. Use Save & Close in the overlay to write settings and return to the game.
